selection_scope.goal: Curate a diverse, non-exhaustive set of Ubuntu 24.04 repository packages whose runtime behavior directly depends on libarchive.
selection_policy: Prefer direct binary-package dependencies on libarchive13t64 in Ubuntu 24.04.
Prefer packages from different domains: archive managers, desktop helpers, FUSE tools, media and document plugins, firmware update tooling, package management, libraries, and language bindings.
Describe the runtime behavior that exercises libarchive, not just the fact that the package depends on it.
notes: This file focuses on direct runtime dependents because those are the easiest to verify concretely from Noble package metadata and package source.
The set is intentionally diverse rather than exhaustive.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Uses a libarchive-backed archive class to list, extract, create, update, rename, and remove entries for archive formats handled by libarchive.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: file-roller depends on libarchive13t64 and advertises browse, extract, create, and modify archive support.
file-roller HACKING says src/fr-archive-libarchive.c adds support for archives handled by the libarchive library.
file-roller src/fr-archive-libarchive.c uses archive_read_* and archive_write_* APIs for listing, extraction, and archive updates.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Handles most of Ark's built-in archive browsing, extraction, creation, and modification paths for libarchive-supported formats such as tar-family archives, rpm, deb, and cab files.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: ark depends on libarchive13t64 and describes browse, extract, create, and modify archive support.
Ark documentation says libarchive 3.1+ is required for most archive types, including tar, compressed tar, rpm, deb, and cab.
Ark source ships a dedicated libarchive plugin under plugins/libarchive/.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Mounts supported archives as a FUSE filesystem so other programs can browse and access archive contents without manual extraction.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: archivemount depends on libarchive13t64.
Ubuntu package description says it mounts archives for read/write access as a filesystem and lists libarchive-style formats such as tar, cpio, ISO9660, zip, and shar.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Adds comic-book archive document support to zathura, letting the viewer open comic archives through a libarchive-backed plugin.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: zathura-cb depends on libarchive13t64.
Ubuntu package description identifies it as zathura's comic book archive plugin.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Lets Kodi browse and read archive contents through a libarchive-based VFS add-on.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: kodi-vfs-libarchive depends on libarchive13t64.
Ubuntu package description explicitly says it is a Kodi VFS add-on to support archives and then enumerates libarchive's supported formats and filters.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Reads EPUB ZIP containers to pull metadata and image assets needed to generate Nautilus thumbnails for EPUB and MOBI books.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: gnome-epub-thumbnailer depends on libarchive13t64 and describes itself as a thumbnailer for EPUB and MOBI books.
gnome-epub-thumbnailer src/gnome-epub-thumbnailer.c opens the book with archive_read_support_format_zip(), iterates entries, and reads matching files from inside the archive.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Provides EPUB document access by opening the EPUB ZIP container, listing archive members, reading arbitrary entries, and locating META-INF/container.xml.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: libgepub-0.7-0 depends on libarchive13t64 and is described as a library to read EPUB files.
libgepub libgepub/gepub-archive.c uses archive_read_support_format_zip(), archive_read_next_header(), and archive_read_data() to enumerate and read EPUB members.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Gives GNOME applications safe, automatic archive extraction and archive creation backed directly by libarchive.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: libgnome-autoar-0-0 depends on libarchive13t64 and describes itself as archive integration support for GNOME.
gnome-autoar README says it provides functions and widgets for GNOME applications that want to use archives.
gnome-autoar source uses archive_read_* in gnome-autoar/autoar-extractor.c and archive_write_* in gnome-autoar/autoar-compressor.c.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Parses cabinet-based firmware update payloads, extracts embedded firmware blobs, and reads update metadata from archive contents during firmware update workflows.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: fwupd depends on libarchive13t64.
fwupd src/fu-cabinet-common.c says fu_cabinet_build_silo creates an AppStream silo from a cabinet archive.
fwupd libfwupdplugin/fu-archive.c wraps archive_read_* and archive_write_* APIs for in-memory archive parsing and writing.
fwupd plugin README files, such as plugins/wacom-usb/README.md, describe the daemon decompressing cabinet archives and extracting firmware blobs.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Opens package archives, reads package metadata such as .PKGINFO and .MTREE, builds file lists, and extracts package payloads during package installation and inspection.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: pacman-package-manager depends on libarchive13t64.
pacman lib/libalpm/util.c uses archive_read_new(), archive_read_support_filter_all(), archive_read_support_format_all(), and archive_read_extract() to open and unpack package archives.
pacman lib/libalpm/be_package.c walks archive entries to parse .PKGINFO, .MTREE, and the packaged file list.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Uses libarchive to inspect archive members and report metadata such as embedded filenames and archive format names to libextractor consumers.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: libextractor-plugin-archive depends on libarchive13t64 and describes itself as the archive plugin.
The package description lists the archive formats supported by the libarchive-based plugin.

libextractor src/plugins/archive_extractor.c uses archive_read_support_filter_all(), archive_read_support_format_all(), archive_read_next_header(), archive_entry_pathname(), and archive_format_name().

dependents.runtime_functionality: Exposes libarchive archive reading, extraction, and archive writing to Python code through ctypes wrappers such as file_reader(), extract_file(), and file_writer().
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: python3-libarchive-c depends on libarchive13t64 and describes itself as a Python3 interface to libarchive.
python-libarchive-c README shows runtime examples for extract_file(), file_reader(), and file_writer().
python-libarchive-c libarchive/read.py and libarchive/write.py open archives and writers through libarchive-backed FFI calls.
